solution.p: The opposite action to multiplication is division. When we divide by a negative number, the inequality sign must change direction.
So in set-builder notation, the solution set is {\{ M \mid M \le 4 \}}\text{.}
We can graph this by shading all numbers less than or equal to 4\text{.} Since the sign in the inequality allows for M to equal 4\text{,} we use a bracket at 4\text{.}
We read the graph from left to right to tell us the interval notation for the solution set: {\left(-\infty ,4\right]}\text{.}
